--- title: "Unit 1: Trigonometry" number-sections: true number-depth: 2 execute: echo: false --- ## 🎯 Learning Targets ::: lt-grid ::: lt-card ::: {.lt-badge .c1} LT1 ::: ::: lt-title Label Right-Angled Triangles ::: ::: lt-desc Identify and correctly label the hypotenuse, opposite side, and adjacent side relative to a given angle in a right-angled triangle. ::: ::: lt-skills [Hypotenuse]{.lt-skill .c1} [Opposite & adjacent]{.lt-skill .c1} [Angle reference]{.lt-skill .c1} ::: ::: ::: lt-card ::: {.lt-badge .c2} LT2 ::: ::: lt-title Recall the Trigonometric Ratios ::: ::: lt-desc Define and recall sin θ, cos θ, and tan θ as ratios of sides using SOH-CAH-TOA, and identify the correct ratio for a given problem. ::: ::: lt-skills [SOH-CAH-TOA]{.lt-skill .c2} [Ratio selection]{.lt-skill .c2} [Fraction form]{.lt-skill .c2} ::: ::: ::: lt-card ::: {.lt-badge .c3} LT3 ::: ::: lt-title Find a Missing Side Length ::: ::: lt-desc Use a trigonometric ratio to set up and solve an equation to find an unknown side length, given one side and one acute angle. ::: ::: lt-skills [Equation set-up]{.lt-skill .c3} [Multiply & divide]{.lt-skill .c3} [Calculator use]{.lt-skill .c3} ::: ::: ::: lt-card ::: {.lt-badge .c4} LT4 ::: ::: lt-title Find a Missing Angle ::: ::: lt-desc Use inverse trigonometric functions (sin⁻¹, cos⁻¹, tan⁻¹) to find an unknown angle when two sides of a right-angled triangle are known. ::: ::: lt-skills [Inverse trig]{.lt-skill .c4} [Two-side ratio]{.lt-skill .c4} [Degree rounding]{.lt-skill .c4} ::: ::: ::: lt-card ::: {.lt-badge .c5} LT5 ::: ::: lt-title Solve Elevation & Depression Problems ::: ::: lt-desc Interpret diagrams and written descriptions involving angles of elevation and depression, and apply trigonometry to find distances and heights. ::: ::: lt-skills [Elevation & depression]{.lt-skill .c5} [Diagram reading]{.lt-skill .c5} [Real-world modelling]{.lt-skill .c5} ::: ::: ::: lt-card ::: {.lt-badge .c1} LT6 ::: ::: lt-title Solve Multi-step Problems ::: ::: lt-desc Combine two or more trigonometric steps, or integrate trigonometry with other geometric knowledge, to solve complex problems. ::: ::: lt-skills [Multi-step reasoning]{.lt-skill .c1} [Combined geometry]{.lt-skill .c1} [Exact & rounded answers]{.lt-skill .c1} ::: ::: ::: ## 🗂️ Formula Card :::::::::::::::::::: formula-card ### Trigonometry — Right-Angled Triangles {.unnumbered} --- #### Labelling the Triangle {.unnumbered} ::: sticky-note-yellow **💡 Key Rule** The labels **opposite**, **adjacent**, and **hypotenuse** depend on which angle **θ** you are working from. The **hypotenuse** is always opposite the right angle — it never changes. ::: ::: rule-group **Three sides relative to angle θ** ```{=html} ``` - **Hypotenuse (H)** — longest side, always opposite the right angle - **Opposite (O)** — the side directly across from angle θ - **Adjacent (A)** — the side next to θ (not the hypotenuse) ::: --- #### SOH-CAH-TOA {.unnumbered} ::: sticky-note-blue **🎯 Memory Trick** — **S**ome **O**ld **H**ouses **C**an **A**lways **H**ide **T**heir **O**ld **A**ge ::: ::: rule-group **The three ratios** $$\sin\theta = \frac{O}{H} \qquad \cos\theta = \frac{A}{H} \qquad \tan\theta = \frac{O}{A}$$ **Choosing the right ratio** — identify which two sides are involved (known + unknown), then pick the ratio that contains both of them. | Known & Unknown | Ratio to use | |---|---| | Opp and Hyp | **sin** | | Adj and Hyp | **cos** | | Opp and Adj | **tan** | ::: --- #### Finding a Missing Side {.unnumbered} ::: sticky-note-pink **⚠️ Common Mistake** Check whether the unknown is on the **top** or **bottom** before rearranging. $x = H \times \sin\theta$ is very different from $x = \dfrac{O}{\sin\theta}$. ::: ::: rule-group **Unknown on top** — multiply $$\sin\theta = \frac{x}{H} \implies x = H \times \sin\theta$$ $$\cos\theta = \frac{x}{H} \implies x = H \times \cos\theta$$ $$\tan\theta = \frac{x}{A} \implies x = A \times \tan\theta$$ **Unknown on bottom** — divide $$\sin\theta = \frac{O}{x} \implies x = \frac{O}{\sin\theta}$$ $$\cos\theta = \frac{A}{x} \implies x = \frac{A}{\cos\theta}$$ $$\tan\theta = \frac{O}{x} \implies x = \frac{O}{\tan\theta}$$ **Example** — find $x$ given hypotenuse = 10 cm, θ = 30°: $$\sin 30° = \frac{x}{10} \implies x = 10 \times \sin 30° = 5 \text{ cm}$$ ::: --- #### Finding a Missing Angle {.unnumbered} ::: sticky-note-orange **📝 Key Idea** Use the **inverse** trig function to undo the ratio. On your calculator: press **SHIFT** (or **2nd**) then **sin / cos / tan**. ::: ::: rule-group **The three inverse functions** $$\theta = \sin^{-1}\!\left(\frac{O}{H}\right)$$ $$\theta = \cos^{-1}\!\left(\frac{A}{H}\right)$$ $$\theta = \tan^{-1}\!\left(\frac{O}{A}\right)$$ **Example** — find θ given opposite = 5 cm, hypotenuse = 13 cm: $$\sin\theta = \frac{5}{13} \implies \theta = \sin^{-1}\!\left(\frac{5}{13}\right) \approx 22.6°$$ ::: --- #### Angles of Elevation & Depression {.unnumbered} ::: sticky-note-yellow **💡 Key Fact** Both angles are measured from the **horizontal**. They are **equal** (alternate angles) when the ground is level. ::: ::: rule-group **Elevation** — angle measured *upward* from horizontal ```{=html} ``` $$\tan\theta = \frac{h}{d} \qquad h = d\tan\theta \qquad d = \frac{h}{\tan\theta}$$ **Depression** — angle measured *downward* from horizontal ```{=html} ``` $$\tan\theta = \frac{h}{d} \qquad \text{(same formula — alternate angles)}$$ ::: --- #### Angle Sum Reminder {.unnumbered} ::: rule-group In any right-angled triangle the two acute angles sum to 90°: $$\alpha + \beta = 90° \implies \beta = 90° - \alpha$$ ::: :::::::::::::::::::: ## 📝 Practice Problems :::::::::: panel-tabset ### 🟢 Easy ::::: {#exr-trig-1} *Label the hypotenuse, opposite, and adjacent sides relative to the marked angle.* Triangle $UDA$ has a right angle at $U$ and angle $\theta$ at $D$. ::: {.content-visible when-format="html"} ::: **(a)** Which side is the hypotenuse? **(b)** Which side is opposite to angle θ? **(c)** Which side is adjacent to angle θ? ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution ::: {.content-visible when-format="html"} ::: **a)** $$ \begin{aligned} \text{Hyp = DA (opposite right angle at U)} \end{aligned} $$ **b)** $$ \begin{aligned} \text{Opp = UA (across from } \theta \text{ at D)} \end{aligned} $$ **c)** $$ \begin{aligned} \text{Adj = UD (next to } \theta \text{, not hyp)} \end{aligned} $$ ::: ::: exercise-meta
Hypotenuse identification Opposite side Adjacent side
::: ::::: ::::: {#exr-trig-2} *Write the trigonometric ratio as a fraction.* The diagram shows a right-angled triangle. ::: {.content-visible when-format="html"} ::: **(a)** Write $\sin\,45°$ as a fraction. ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution ::: {.content-visible when-format="html"} ::: **a)** $$ \begin{aligned} \sin\theta = \dfrac{\text{Opp}}{\text{Hyp}} = \dfrac{8}{11.31} \end{aligned} $$ ::: ::: exercise-meta
SOH-CAH-TOA Fraction form Ratio identification
::: ::::: ::::: {#exr-trig-3} *State which trigonometric ratio should be used and write the equation.* In a right triangle, $\theta=30°$, opposite=13 cm, adjacent=6 cm. ::: {.content-visible when-format="html"} ::: **(a)** State the correct ratio and write the equation. ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution ::: {.content-visible when-format="html"} ::: **a)** $$ \begin{aligned} \text{Opp and Adj} \Rightarrow \tan\theta = \dfrac{13}{6} \end{aligned} $$ ::: ::: exercise-meta
SOH-CAH-TOA Ratio selection Side identification
::: ::::: ::::: {#exr-trig-4} *Find the length of the unknown side. Give your answer to 2 decimal places.* Hypotenuse = 5 cm, θ = 55°. ::: {.content-visible when-format="html"} ::: **(a)** Find $x$. ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution ::: {.content-visible when-format="html"} ::: **a)** $$ \begin{aligned} \sin 55° = \dfrac{x}{5} \\ x = 5 \times \sin 55° \\ x = 4.1 \text{ cm} \end{aligned} $$ ::: ::: exercise-meta
SOH-CAH-TOA Equation set-up Calculator use
::: ::::: ::::: {#exr-trig-5} *Find the length of the unknown side. Give your answer to 2 decimal places.* Hypotenuse = 6 cm, θ = 25°. ::: {.content-visible when-format="html"} ::: **(a)** Find $x$. ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution ::: {.content-visible when-format="html"} ::: **a)** $$ \begin{aligned} \cos 25° = \dfrac{x}{6} \\ x = 6 \times \cos 25° \\ x = 5.44 \text{ cm} \end{aligned} $$ ::: ::: exercise-meta
SOH-CAH-TOA Equation set-up Calculator use
::: ::::: ::::: {#exr-trig-6} *Find the length of the unknown side. Give your answer to 2 decimal places.* Adjacent = 8 cm, θ = 40°. ::: {.content-visible when-format="html"} ::: **(a)** Find $x$. ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution ::: {.content-visible when-format="html"} ::: **a)** $$ \begin{aligned} \tan 40° = \dfrac{x}{8} \\ x = 8 \times \tan 40° \\ x = 6.71 \text{ cm} \end{aligned} $$ ::: ::: exercise-meta
SOH-CAH-TOA Equation set-up Calculator use
::: ::::: ::: math-sep ∑ × π ÷ √ ◆ ∞ ± ∫ ≠ Δ ::: ### 🟡 Medium ::::: {#exr-trig-7} *Find the length of the hypotenuse. Give your answer to 2 decimal places.* Opposite = 14 cm, θ = 65°. ::: {.content-visible when-format="html"} ::: **(a)** Find $x$. ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution ::: {.content-visible when-format="html"} ::: **a)** $$ \begin{aligned} \sin 65° = \dfrac{14}{x} \\ x = \dfrac{14}{\sin 65°} \\ x = 15.45 \text{ cm} \end{aligned} $$ ::: ::: exercise-meta
SOH-CAH-TOA Rearranging equations Calculator use
::: ::::: ::::: {#exr-trig-8} *Find the length of the hypotenuse. Give your answer to 2 decimal places.* Adjacent = 13 cm, θ = 25°. ::: {.content-visible when-format="html"} ::: **(a)** Find $x$. ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution ::: {.content-visible when-format="html"} ::: **a)** $$ \begin{aligned} \cos 25° = \dfrac{13}{x} \\ x = \dfrac{13}{\cos 25°} \\ x = 14.34 \text{ cm} \end{aligned} $$ ::: ::: exercise-meta
SOH-CAH-TOA Rearranging equations Calculator use
::: ::::: ::::: {#exr-trig-9} *Find the length of the unknown side. Give your answer to 2 decimal places.* Opposite = 15 cm, θ = 40°. ::: {.content-visible when-format="html"} ::: **(a)** Find $x$. ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution ::: {.content-visible when-format="html"} ::: **a)** $$ \begin{aligned} \tan 40° = \dfrac{15}{x} \\ x = \dfrac{15}{\tan 40°} \\ x = 17.88 \text{ cm} \end{aligned} $$ ::: ::: exercise-meta
SOH-CAH-TOA Rearranging equations Calculator use
::: ::::: ::::: {#exr-trig-10} *Find the size of angle θ. Give your answer to 1 decimal place.* Opposite = 9.97 cm, hypotenuse = 11 cm. ::: {.content-visible when-format="html"} ::: **(a)** Find angle $\theta$. ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution ::: {.content-visible when-format="html"} ::: **a)** $$ \begin{aligned} \sin\theta = \dfrac{9.97}{11} \\ \theta = \sin^{-1}\!\left(\dfrac{9.97}{11}\right) \\ \theta = 65.0° \end{aligned} $$ ::: ::: exercise-meta
Inverse trig functions Two-side ratio Degree rounding
::: ::::: ::::: {#exr-trig-11} *Find the size of angle θ. Give your answer to 1 decimal place.* Adjacent = 9.19 cm, hypotenuse = 12 cm. ::: {.content-visible when-format="html"} ::: **(a)** Find angle $\theta$. ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution ::: {.content-visible when-format="html"} ::: **a)** $$ \begin{aligned} \cos\theta = \dfrac{9.19}{12} \\ \theta = \cos^{-1}\!\left(\dfrac{9.19}{12}\right) \\ \theta = 40.0° \end{aligned} $$ ::: ::: exercise-meta
Inverse trig functions Two-side ratio Degree rounding
::: ::::: ::::: {#exr-trig-12} *Find the size of angle θ. Give your answer to 1 decimal place.* Opposite = 5.0 cm, adjacent = 5 cm. ::: {.content-visible when-format="html"} ::: **(a)** Find angle $\theta$. ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution ::: {.content-visible when-format="html"} ::: **a)** $$ \begin{aligned} \tan\theta = \dfrac{5.0}{5} \\ \theta = \tan^{-1}\!\left(\dfrac{5.0}{5}\right) \\ \theta = 45.0° \end{aligned} $$ ::: ::: exercise-meta
Inverse trig functions Two-side ratio Degree rounding
::: ::::: ::::: {#exr-trig-13} *Use trigonometry to find the unknown measurement. Give your answer to 2 decimal places.* A person stands 50 m from a vertical tower. The angle of elevation to the top is 55°. ::: {.content-visible when-format="html"} ::: **(a)** Find the height of the tower. ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution ::: {.content-visible when-format="html"} ::: **a)** $$ \begin{aligned} \tan 55° = \dfrac{h}{50} \\ h = 50 \times \tan 55° \\ h = 71.41 \text{ m} \end{aligned} $$ ::: ::: exercise-meta
Elevation diagram tan ratio Real-world modelling
::: ::::: ::::: {#exr-trig-14} *Use trigonometry to find the unknown measurement. Give your answer to 2 decimal places.* An observer at the top of a 40 m cliff looks down at a boat with angle of depression 30°. ::: {.content-visible when-format="html"} ::: **(a)** Find the horizontal distance from the cliff base to the boat. ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution ::: {.content-visible when-format="html"} ::: **a)** $$ \begin{aligned} \tan 30° = \dfrac{40}{d} \\ d = \dfrac{40}{\tan 30°} \\ d = 69.28 \text{ m} \end{aligned} $$ ::: ::: exercise-meta
Depression diagram tan ratio Alternate angles
::: ::::: ::::: {#exr-trig-17} *Show all working. Give your answer to 2 decimal places.* A flagpole casts a shadow of 5.72 m when the angle of elevation to the sun is 65°. ::: {.content-visible when-format="html"} ::: **(a)** Find the height of the flagpole. ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution ::: {.content-visible when-format="html"} ::: **a)** $$ \begin{aligned} \tan 65° = \dfrac{h}{5.72} \\ h = 5.72 \times \tan 65° = 12.27 \text{ m} \end{aligned} $$ ::: ::: exercise-meta
tan ratio Shadow geometry Real-world modelling
::: ::::: ::::: {#exr-trig-18} *Show all working. Give your answer to 2 decimal places.* A hill is inclined at 12° to the horizontal, base at sea level. **(a)** If you walk 150 m up the hill, what is your height above sea level? **(b)** If you climb to 80 m above sea level, how far have you walked? ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution **a)** $$ \begin{aligned} h = 150 \sin 12° = 31.19 \text{ m} \end{aligned} $$ **b)** $$ \begin{aligned} \sin 12° = \dfrac{80}{d} \\ d = \dfrac{80}{\sin 12°} = 384.78 \text{ m} \end{aligned} $$ ::: ::: exercise-meta
sin for height cos for horizontal Hill geometry
::: ::::: ::::: {#exr-trig-19} *Show all working. Give your answer to 2 decimal places.* A train climbs 6.4 m for every 150 m of track. **(a)** Find the angle of incline. ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution **a)** $$ \begin{aligned} \sin\theta = \dfrac{6.4}{150} \\ \theta = \sin^{-1}\!\left(\dfrac{6.4}{150}\right) = 2.4° \end{aligned} $$ ::: ::: exercise-meta
sin⁻¹ Gradient geometry Small angles
::: ::::: ::::: {#exr-trig-20} *Show all working. Give your answer to 1 decimal place.* A 65 m building. Point $A$ is 113 m from its base. **(a)** Find the angle of elevation from A to the top of the building. **(b)** What is the angle of depression from the top to A? ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution **a)** $$ \begin{aligned} \tan\theta = \dfrac{65}{113} \\ \theta = \tan^{-1}\!\left(\dfrac{65}{113}\right) = 29.9° \end{aligned} $$ **b)** $$ \begin{aligned} \text{Depression angle} = \text{elevation angle} = 29.9° \\ \text{(Alternate interior angles with horizontal)} \end{aligned} $$ ::: ::: exercise-meta
Elevation angle Depression angle Alternate angles
::: ::::: ::::: {#exr-trig-21} *Show all working. Give your answer to 2 decimal places.* The handle is held 85 cm above the ground. The string is 10 m long at 55° to the horizontal. **Given:** \text{String} = 10\text{ m} · \text{Angle} = 55° · \text{Handle} = 85\text{ cm} ::: {.content-visible when-format="html"} ::: **(a)** Calculate the height of the kite above the ground. ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution ::: {.content-visible when-format="html"} ::: **a)** $$ \begin{aligned} \text{Vertical} = 10 \sin 55° = 8.19 \text{ m} \\ \text{Handle} = 85 \text{ cm} = 0.85 \text{ m} \\ \text{Total} = 8.19 + 0.85 = 9.04 \text{ m} \end{aligned} $$ ::: ::: exercise-meta
sin ratio Height calculation Unit conversion
::: ::::: ::::: {#exr-trig-24} *Show all working. Give angles to 1 decimal place.* A ladder leans against a wall. Wall height = 5.6 m, base distance = 0.9 m. Safe angle: 70°–80° to the ground. ::: {.content-visible when-format="html"} ::: **(a)** Is the ladder safe? Show your working. **(b)** Calculate the length of the ladder. ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution ::: {.content-visible when-format="html"} ::: **a)** $$ \begin{aligned} \tan\theta = \dfrac{5.6}{0.9} \\ \theta = \tan^{-1}\!\left(\dfrac{5.6}{0.9}\right) = 80.9° \\ 80.9° \text{ is NOT safe (outside 70°–80°)} \end{aligned} $$ **b)** $$ \begin{aligned} L^2 = 5.6^2 + 0.9^2 = 32.17 \\ L = \sqrt{32.17} = 5.67 \text{ m} \end{aligned} $$ ::: ::: exercise-meta
tan ratio Angle calculation Safety constraint
::: ::::: ::::: {#exr-trig-25} *Convert units where needed. Give your answer to 2 decimal places.* A ramp: horizontal distance 2.0 m, height 20 cm. ::: {.content-visible when-format="html"} ::: **(a)** Calculate the size of angle $y$. ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution ::: {.content-visible when-format="html"} ::: **a)** $$ \begin{aligned} \text{Convert: } 20 \text{ cm} = 0.2 \text{ m} \\ \tan y = \dfrac{0.2}{2.0} \\ y = \tan^{-1}\!\left(\dfrac{0.2}{2.0}\right) = 5.7° \end{aligned} $$ ::: ::: exercise-meta
Unit conversion tan⁻¹ Ramp geometry
::: ::::: ::: math-sep ∑ × π ÷ √ ◆ ∞ ± ∫ ≠ Δ ::: ### 🔴 Challenging ::::: {#exr-trig-15} *Draw a diagram, label all measurements, and find the answer. Give your answer to 2 decimal places.* A tree of height 20 m is observed at an angle of elevation of 40° from the ground. ::: {.content-visible when-format="html"} ::: **(a)** Draw and label a diagram. **(b)** Find the horizontal distance to 2 d.p. ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution ::: {.content-visible when-format="html"} ::: **a)** $$ \begin{aligned} \text{Vertical = height, horizontal = distance, }\theta\text{ at base} \end{aligned} $$ **b)** $$ \begin{aligned} \tan 40° = \dfrac{20}{d} \\ d = \dfrac{20}{\tan 40°} \\ d = 23.84 \text{ m} \end{aligned} $$ ::: ::: exercise-meta
Problem decomposition Diagram construction tan ratio
::: ::::: ::::: {#exr-trig-16} *Draw a diagram, label all measurements, and find the answer. Give your answer to 2 decimal places.* A drone hovers 20 m above ground. Angle of depression to a landmark is 30°. ::: {.content-visible when-format="html"} ::: **(a)** Draw and label a diagram. **(b)** Find the horizontal distance to 2 d.p. ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution ::: {.content-visible when-format="html"} ::: **a)** $$ \begin{aligned} \text{Horizontal line at top; depression angle = interior angle} \end{aligned} $$ **b)** $$ \begin{aligned} \text{Depression angle} = 30° \text{ (alternate angles)} \\ \tan 30° = \dfrac{20}{d} \\ d = \dfrac{20}{\tan 30°} = 34.64 \text{ m} \end{aligned} $$ ::: ::: exercise-meta
Problem decomposition Diagram construction Alternate angles
::: ::::: ::::: {#exr-trig-22} *Show all working clearly. Give your answer to 2 decimal places.* Two points A and B on opposite sides of a tower. From A: elevation 30°, distance 15 m. From B: elevation 35°. **(a)** Find the height of the tower. **(b)** Find the horizontal distance from B to the tower. ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution **a)** $$ \begin{aligned} h = 15 \times \tan 30° = 8.66 \text{ m} \end{aligned} $$ **b)** $$ \begin{aligned} d_B = \dfrac{8.66}{\tan 35°} = 12.37 \text{ m} \end{aligned} $$ ::: ::: exercise-meta
Multi-step reasoning Shared side Combined trig
::: ::::: ::::: {#exr-trig-23} *Show all working clearly. Give your answer to 2 decimal places.* A ship sails 12 km on bearing 060°. **(a)** How far north of port is the ship? **(b)** How far east of port is the ship? ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution **a)** $$ \begin{aligned} N = 12 \cos 60° = 6 \text{ km} \end{aligned} $$ **b)** $$ \begin{aligned} E = 12 \sin 60° = 10.39 \text{ km} \end{aligned} $$ ::: ::: exercise-meta
Bearing interpretation North reference Multi-step trig
::: ::::: ::::: {#exr-trig-26} *Show all working clearly. Give your answer to 2 decimal places.* Two right-angled triangles share side BD. **Given:** AB=8\text{ cm} · BC=2\text{ cm} · \angle BCD=55° **(a)** Calculate angle $ABD$ (marked $x$). ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution **a)** $$ \begin{aligned} \triangle BCD: \quad BD = BC \times \tan 55° = 2 \times \tan 55° = 2.86 \text{ cm} \\ \triangle ABD: \quad \sin(x) = \dfrac{2.86}{8} \\ x = \sin^{-1}\!\left(\dfrac{2.86}{8}\right) = 20.9° \end{aligned} $$ ::: ::: exercise-meta
Multi-step reasoning Vertex labelling Combined trig
::: ::::: ::::: {#exr-trig-27} *Show all working clearly. Give your answer to 2 decimal places.* Right-angled triangles ABD and BCD share side BD. **Given:** AB=16\text{ cm} · \angle ABD=70° · \angle ABD:\angle DBC=5:2 **(a)** Work out the length of $CD$. ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution **a)** $$ \begin{aligned} \angle DBC = \dfrac{2}{5} \times 70° = 28.0° \\ BD = 16 \sin 70° = 15.04 \text{ cm} \\ CD = BD \times \tan 28.0° = 15.04 \times \tan 28.0° = 8 \text{ cm} \end{aligned} $$ ::: ::: exercise-meta
Ratio splitting Multi-step trig Angle arithmetic
::: ::::: ::::: {#exr-trig-28} *Show all working. Give your answer to 1 decimal place.* Isosceles triangle $FLG$: $FL = LG = 32$ cm, $FG = 20$ cm. ::: {.content-visible when-format="html"} ::: **(a)** Work out angle $LFG$. **(b)** Work out angle $FLG$. ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution ::: {.content-visible when-format="html"} ::: **a)** $$ \begin{aligned} \text{Drop perpendicular from L to midpoint M of FG} \\ FM = \dfrac{20}{2} = 10.0 \text{ cm} \\ \sin(\angle LFG) = \dfrac{10.0}{32} \\ \angle LFG = \sin^{-1}\!\left(\dfrac{10.0}{32}\right) = 18.2° \end{aligned} $$ **b)** $$ \begin{aligned} \angle FLG = 180° - 2 \times 18.2° = 143.6° \end{aligned} $$ ::: ::: exercise-meta
Isosceles properties Perpendicular bisector sin application
::: ::::: ::::: {#exr-trig-29} *Show all working. Give bearing to nearest degree.* A helicopter flies 10 km due east, then 6 km due north. **(a)** Find the direct distance. **(b)** Calculate the bearing. ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution **a)** $$ \begin{aligned} d^2 = 10^2 + 6^2 = 136 \\ d = \sqrt{136} = 11.66 \text{ km} \end{aligned} $$ **b)** $$ \begin{aligned} \tan\theta = \dfrac{10}{6} \\ \theta = \tan^{-1}\!\left(\dfrac{10}{6}\right) = 59.0° \\ \text{Bearing} = 059° \end{aligned} $$ ::: ::: exercise-meta
Pythagoras tan⁻¹ for bearing Compass directions
::: ::::: ::::: {#exr-trig-30} *Show all working. Give your answer to 2 decimal places.* A boat approaches a 120 m cliff with a 25 m lighthouse on top. Angle of elevation to top of lighthouse is 20°. **Given:** \text{Cliff} = 120\text{ m} · \text{Lighthouse} = 25\text{ m} · \text{Elevation to lighthouse top} = 20° **(a)** Find the distance from the boat to the base of the cliff. **(b)** Find the angle of elevation from the boat to the top of the cliff. ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution **a)** $$ \begin{aligned} \tan 20° = \dfrac{145}{d} \\ d = \dfrac{145}{\tan 20°} = 398.38 \text{ m} \end{aligned} $$ **b)** $$ \begin{aligned} \tan\alpha = \dfrac{120}{398.38} \\ \alpha = \tan^{-1}\!\left(\dfrac{120}{398.38}\right) = 16.8° \end{aligned} $$ ::: ::: exercise-meta
Multi-step trig Total height Elevation angles
::: ::::: ::::: {#exr-trig-31} *Show all working. Give your answer to 2 decimal places.* A chord of 5.3 cm subtends an angle of 34° at the centre. **Given:** \text{Chord} = 5.3\text{ cm} · \text{Angle at centre} = 34° **(a)** Find the radius of the circle. ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution **a)** $$ \begin{aligned} \text{Half-chord} = \dfrac{5.3}{2} = 2.65 \text{ cm} \\ \sin 34° = \dfrac{2.65}{r} \\ r = \dfrac{2.65}{\sin 34°} = 4.74 \text{ cm} \end{aligned} $$ ::: ::: exercise-meta
Circle geometry sin ratio Right triangle in circle
::: ::::: ::::: {#exr-trig-32} *Show all working. Give your answer to 2 decimal places.* From 200 m away: elevation to flagpole bottom = 34°, to top = 38°. **(a)** Find the height of the flagpole. ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution **a)** $$ \begin{aligned} h_1 = 200 \tan 34° = 134.9 \text{ m} \\ h_2 = 200 \tan 38° = 156.26 \text{ m} \\ \text{Flagpole} = 156.26 - 134.9 = 21.36 \text{ m} \end{aligned} $$ ::: ::: exercise-meta
Multi-step trig Two-angle setup Subtraction
::: ::::: ::::: {#exr-trig-33} *Show all working. Give your answer to 2 decimal places.* Depression from top of 18 m cliff: first 3.3°, then 5.5°. How far does the boat move? **(a)** How much closer must the boat move? ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution **a)** $$ \begin{aligned} d_1 = \dfrac{18}{\tan 3.3°} = 312.18 \text{ m} \\ d_2 = \dfrac{18}{\tan 5.5°} = 186.94 \text{ m} \\ \text{Moved} = 312.18 - 186.94 = 125.24 \text{ m} \end{aligned} $$ ::: ::: exercise-meta
Two depression angles Distance difference Multi-step trig
::: ::::: ::::: {#exr-trig-34} *Show all working. Give your answer to 2 decimal places.* Helicopter at 90 km/h. Takes 15 s to go from overhead to elevation angle 65°. **(a)** Find the height of the helicopter. ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution **a)** $$ \begin{aligned} \text{Distance} = 90 \times \dfrac{15}{3600} = 0.38 \text{ km} \\ h = 0.38 \times \tan 65° = 0.81 \text{ km} \\ h = 810 \text{ m} \end{aligned} $$ ::: ::: exercise-meta
